hero.heading: @love-rox/tcy-rehype
hero.tagline: A rehype plugin that slots into a `unified` HAST pipeline and applies tate-chu-yoko at build time. Markdown, MDX, plain HTML ‚Äî all without a client runtime.

install.heading: Install
install.description: `@love-rox/tcy-core` comes along as a dependency. Use this on any `unified`-based build (remark / rehype / Astro / eleventy / ‚Ä¶). If you specifically want Astro ergonomics, the [Astro adapter](./astro) is purpose-built.

usage.heading: Markdown pipeline
usage.description: Slot `rehypeTcy` in between `remark-rehype` and `rehype-stringify` (or wherever your pipeline finalizes HAST). The wrapping is baked into the HTML before it leaves the build.
usage.htmlOnlyDescription: If there's no Markdown in the picture ‚Äî just HTML in, HTML out ‚Äî it's the same idea:

options.heading: Options
options.description: The shared options ‚Äî `target`, `combine`, `include`, `exclude`, `maxLength`, `excludeWords` ‚Äî behave exactly as they do in the React, Vue, and Astro adapters.

behavior.heading: Behavior notes
items: **Idempotent**. Running the plugin twice on the same HAST gives the same output as running it once ‚Äî no doubled spans.
The default `skipTags` (`code` / `pre` / `script` / `style`) keeps code samples and embedded JSON safe from accidental wrapping.
Runs are not joined across element boundaries ‚Äî `1234` produces two separate spans.
Runs filtered out by `maxLength` or `excludeWords` are demoted back to plain text and merged into the surrounding text segment automatically.

related.heading: If you're on Astro
related.description: If you're on Astro 4+ and dealing with `.md` / `.mdx` / `.astro`, the [Astro integration](./astro) wires up the Markdown pipeline and ships a `<Tcy>` component for `.astro` files ‚Äî fewer moving parts to assemble yourself.
